title: Linked code changes now stale memory packets
summary: Kage stores source file fingerprints on new and diff-derived memory, flags linked-code drift, and now exposes reconciliation as agent-owned work through context, Stop hooks, CLI/MCP, and PR checks.
body: Kage stores source file fingerprints on newly captured and diff-derived memory packets and skips .agent_memory paths to avoid self-referential churn. When linked source hashes drift, Kage reports agent-owned memory reconciliation through kage_context, kage_memory_reconcile/kage reconcile, Claude Code Stop hooks, and kage pr check. The agent must update, supersede, or mark stale memory before final handoff instead of pushing an inbox chore to the user. Existing older packets without the strict fingerprint policy are not retroactively marked from broad file hashes.
